reason: Both answers correctly land on talking the team out of full event sourcing, recommend an append-only ledger table for the audit-trail benefit, plain CRUD for subscriptions, and suggest evaluating an existing billing provider before building custom. Both correctly flag event/schema versioning and eventual-consistency UX problems as billing-specific pain points, and both are concise and well-structured. Neither explicitly names the enthusiasm-for-the-pattern-not-the-problem red flag, though B gestures at it near the end. Overall the two are comparable in correctness and usefulness, with only minor, roughly offsetting differences in emphasis.
